summary: Round 1’s direct symlink/path escapes and markdown `innerHTML` XSS are closed, but the file API still has a check/use symlink race that can reopen both read and write confinement under concurrent filesystem changes.

findings.title: Root confinement is still bypassable via symlink swap between validation and filesystem use
findings.recommendation: `assertRealInRoot` and `assertWriteInRoot` validate a pathname, then later `readdir`/`stat`/`readFile`/`mkdir`/`writeFile` reopen that pathname. A local attacker who can mutate the tree can replace the checked path or an intermediate parent with a symlink after the guard runs, causing the subsequent operation to follow the new link outside `ROOT`. To actually close the symlink class, perform the operation through handles opened with no-follow semantics (`O_NOFOLLOW`/directory FDs) and validate the opened handle, or reject symlinks on every traversed component immediately before each use and avoid reopening by pathname.
